mechanism_narrative: Persistently high unemployment above 9% signals weak aggregate demand and consumer confidence due to slack in the labor market, leading to lower corporate earnings expectations and negative equity returns over the next quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:Post-GFC recovery characterized by a jobless recovery narrative
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when the unemployment rate is above 9.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A near-zero federal funds rate reduces the discount rate for future corporate cash flows and encourages risk-taking by investors seeking yield (TINA - 'There Is No Alternative'), boosting equity prices over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:ZIRP (Zero Interest Rate Policy) in effect since December 2008
failure_condition: The signal's rank information coefficient (IC) for 20-day returns is negative over any 3-year rolling window while FEDFUNDS is below 1%.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales show accelerating growth while housing starts remain depressed, it indicates consumer spending is shifting towards goods and services away from housing investment. This benefits S&P 500 companies more directly than residential construction, leading to positive returns.
candidate_trigger: 3-month change in RSAFS > 0 and 3-month change in HOUST <= 0
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending recovering faster than housing market post-GFC
failure_condition: A strategy based on the interaction of RSAFS momentum and HOUST weakness fails to outperform a simple RSAFS momentum strategy over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in industrial production reflects strengthening business activity and improving corporate revenues. This leading indicator of economic health translates into higher expected earnings and positive equity market performance over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production is a key component of the Index of Coincident Economic Indicators
failure_condition: The 6-month change in INDPRO has a statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) or negative relationship with 60-day forward S&P 500 returns over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a ZIRP environment, an unexpected increase in inflation creates fears that the central bank will be forced to tighten policy sooner than anticipated, leading to a rapid repricing of risk assets and negative short-term returns.
candidate_trigger: Month-over-month change in CPIAUCSL > 0.4% and FEDFUNDS < 1.0%
evidence_refs: fact:Markets are highly sensitive to inflation data during ZIRP
failure_condition: The average 5-day S&P 500 return following an inflation surprise (as defined by the trigger) is not statistically significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating nonfarm payrolls growth is a direct, high-frequency indicator of a strengthening economy, leading to upward revisions in corporate earnings forecasts and driving positive equity returns over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS is a widely watched coincident indicator of economic activity
failure_condition: The 3-month change in PAYEMS fails to exhibit a persistent positive rank correlation (Spearman's rho > 0.05) with 60-day forward S&P 500 returns.

mechanism_narrative: A quarter-over-quarter contraction in real GDP signals a significant economic slowdown or recession, triggering broad-based earnings estimate cuts and causing investors to de-risk portfolios, leading to negative returns.
failure_condition: In quarters following a negative real GDP print, the average 60-day forward S&P 500 return is not statistically different from the average return in positive growth quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid improvement in the labor market (falling UNRATE) while monetary policy remains extremely accommodative (low FEDFUNDS) is the strongest signal of a durable recovery, leading to sustained positive equity returns as fears of a double-dip recession fade.
evidence_refs: fact:Dual mandate of the Federal Reserve links employment and price stability
failure_condition: The combination of a falling UNRATE and low FEDFUNDS does not predict positive 60-day returns with an accuracy significantly above 50% in a walk-forward test.
